Dear Mr. Davidian:
CAIB is a membership organization which among other things
lobbies for Emall business at the state capital. 
Several years ago'
CAtB, working with the FPPC establ ished the distinction 
between
MEMBERS of lobbying associations Iike CAIB members and Iobbyist
employers. Legal ly, the members of an association are not
required to f ill 
out the PaPer work of Iobbyist employers.
At that time, in the late 197O's, the maximum contribution
which a member could give was $5OO Per year if the member was to
maintain the distinction 
of not being a lobbyist employer. tle have
been informed by one of your auditor that the number has been
changed to $5,OOO per year. 
We would like you to verify our
understanding and to explain to us how the rules work.
We would I ike you specifical ly to address:
1. Does the $5,ooo timit cover total contributions 
to ALL
associations, or to each individual association. 
(Example:
a business may be a member of several trade associations
which lobby in Sacramento. )
2. Are there any other pertinent points which we should be
aware of in this area.
Sincerely
ames P.

sections.question:
1. Does the $5,000 limit cover total contributions to ALL associations, or to each individual association. (Example: a business may be a member of several trade associations which lobby in Sacramento.)
2. Are there any other pertinent points which we should be aware of in this area.
sections.question_synthetic: The California Association of Independent Business seeks clarification on whether the $5,000 annual contribution limit for members of lobbying associations applies to total contributions across all associations or to each individual association separately, and requests information on other relevant rules governing member contributions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. Document contains two specific numbered questions but no conclusion section. Dated November 20, 1991. Sender is James P. Gass, President of CAIB. No FPPC advice or conclusion provided in this document.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1. Does the $5,000 limit cover total contributions to ALL associations, or to each individual association. (Example: a business may be a member of several trade associations which lobby in Sacramento.)
2. Are there any other pertinent points which we should be aware of in this area.

embedding.summary: CAIB writes to the FPPC seeking clarification on contribution limits for members of lobbying associations, specifically whether a $5,000 annual limit applies globally or per-association and requesting explanation of related rules.
